cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.title: Market & consumer trends to watch in 2024 - Cold brew coffee
cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.content:
Based in San Diego, CA, Trident Coffee Roasters offers nitro cold brew, espresso, and whole roasted beans direct to consumers and wholesale. This is done online and through the brand’s three cold-brew “tap-rooms”. By contrast, Finlays, founded in 1750, is a leading independent B2B manufacturer and supplier of coffee, tea, and botanicals worldwide.

Speaking from these highly diverse perspectives, Eric and Stephanie provide valuable insights into technical developments, demographics, and cultural trends driving consumer preferences for cold brew and ready-to-drink (RTD) coffee. [...] Eric, who has over 13 years of combined experience in entrepreneurship, management, specialty coffee, agrees. “It’s still definitely a younger demographic. Millennials and Gen Z appreciate the craft component. They’re interested in finding new experiences, and what we’re bringing to the table is a new coffee experience.”

### A focus on the supposed health benefits of cold brew

Eric notes that cold brew is gaining popularity “similar to how we saw the craft beer space emerge about 10-15 years ago. There is a lot of focus on ingredients, sourcing, and the type of bean being used,” he says. [...] Paul Clearfire

January 24, 2024 |

Market trends are shaping to make 2024 an exhilarating year for cold brew and ready-to-drink (RTD) coffee. Driven by younger consumers looking for health-conscious alternatives to sugary beverages, the cold brew coffee market is emerging as a place of experimentation, discovery, and craftsmanship.

According to a recent report, the global cold brew coffee market reached a value of about $604.47 million in 2023. Beyond this, it’s expected to reach a value of $3751.76 million by 2032. This impressive growth includes an impressive array of innovations including herbal additives to customise health benefits, and specialised cold brew concentrates for mocktails, cocktails, and other beverages.

cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.title: Cold Brew Coffee Market Size, Share & 2030 Trends Report
cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.content:
By Flavor: Unflavored Foundation Enables Premium Innovation

Unflavored cold brew dominates with a 56.71% market share in 2024, appealing to traditional coffee consumers and establishing brand authenticity. The flavored cold brew segment is growing at a 19.07% CAGR through 2030, driven by consumer demand for diverse taste options and functional ingredients. This growth is particularly driven by younger demographics who experiment with various flavor profiles, from classic vanilla and caramel to exotic fruit and spice infusions. The market growth indicates increasing consumer awareness of cold brew coffee's distinct taste characteristics, which enables companies to develop premium products with diverse flavor combinations. [...] Conventional cold brew holds a 72.12% market share in 2024, primarily due to its widespread retail presence across supermarkets, convenience stores, and coffee shops, along with its competitive price points compared to specialty coffee beverages. The organic segment is growing at a 19.22% CAGR through 2030, as consumers increasingly prioritize products made with pesticide-free coffee beans and environmentally sustainable packaging. The market's expansion demonstrates successful penetration across both value-conscious consumers seeking affordable ready-to-drink options and premium buyers willing to pay more for organic and sustainably sourced products. [...] Off-trade channels hold a 58.34% market share in 2024, as cold brew coffee gains prominence in retail grocery stores, convenience outlets, and online platforms, supporting consumer stockpiling and home consumption. The on-trade channel is growing at a CAGR of 19.36% through 2030, supported by the recovery of the foodservice industry and increasing demand for cold brew coffee in cafes and restaurants. This growth pattern reflects the market's expansion into diverse consumption settings.

cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.title: Cold Brew Coffee Market Size, Share & Trends | Industry Report, 2033
cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.content:
Which is the leading region in the market?

North America led the market in 2024 and is expected to retain its dominance over the forecast period.

What are the future growth trends for this market?

Increasing consumer preference towards cold brew coffee for its smooth and less acidic taste, Rise in the number of specialty coffee shops and The growth in e-commerce platforms selling cold brew coffee are the future growth trends for the cold brew coffee market.

Anantika Sharma

Research Practice Lead [...] For example, in July 2024, Bones Coffee Co. introduced a line of RTD cold brew lattes in flavors like Holy Cannoli and French Toast, available in Walmart stores, catering to the demand for unique, convenient coffee options.

Global coffee chains like Dunkin’ and Costa Coffee have significantly expanded their cold brew offerings in response. On the retail side, private labels and startups are also capitalizing on this trend with ready-to-drink formats available in convenience stores and online. The driver lies in the convergence of premiumization, health consciousness, and convenience, turning cold brew from a seasonal novelty into a daily staple.

## Market Restraint

### High Production Costs and Short Shelf Life Limit Mass Scalability [...] The cold brew coffee market is experiencing a dynamic shift, with brands innovating beyond traditional offerings to meet evolving consumer preferences. Functional ingredients and unique flavors are at the forefront of this transformation. Brands like Califia Farms and La Colombe have introduced cold brews infused with wellness ingredients such as collagen and adaptogens, aligning with the clean-label and plant-based trends.

 For instance, in April 2025, Starbucks launched its Cold Brew with Ginger Honey Citrus as a limited release in Asia, combining cold coffee with traditional Asian flavors to localize appeal.

cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.title: Top trends driving commercial cold brew growth in 2025 - HardTank
cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.content:
It’s fast becoming a real rival to traditional iced coffee, too. In the United Kingdom, up to 43% of coffee consumers.%E2%80%9D) now drink cold brew at least once a month. It proves that for customers, it’s a more appealing option than ever before.

One of the main reasons that commercial cold brew is so appealing is its unique taste. Unlike iced coffee, which is brewed hot and then cooled down, cold brew is steeped in cold water.

The result is a smoother, less acidic, and usually sweeter drink. “A lot of people like the taste of cold brew better than iced coffee due to the stronger component of it,” Sophia explains. [...] Coffee culture is always evolving, and consumer preferences are shifting alongside it. Hot, milk-based coffee drinks are no longer the only option. Now, cold brew coffee is a popular product for specialty coffee shops, as well as restaurants, bakeries, and hotels.

This rapid development isn’t taking place in a vacuum, though. Instead, a number of consumer trends are behind the rise of commercial cold brew. In order to stay competitive, businesses that currently or plan to offer cold brew need to be aware of which shifts are taking place. Among them are changes in flavour preferences, health demands, and convenience requirements from consumers. [...] Cold brew also aligns with the broader trends towards health and wellness. Consumers are increasingly looking for drinks, with 62% of consumers considering health an important factor when buying a drink. This can encompass low-sugar options as well as “clean” sources of energy and traceable, natural ingredients.

Cold brew is often served without milk and sugar, making it well-suited for health-conscious consumers. For this reason, it’s often marketed as a healthier, premium alternative. To meet the rising demand and significant growth, though, you need a deeper understanding of these market trends and tangible ways to meet them.

## How to increase commercial cold brew production without sacrificing quality

cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.title: Why Cold Brew and Nitro Are the Big Coffee Story for Late 2025
cold brew coffee Easter seasonal market.content:
#### Cold coffee is now a year round habit

Cold brew once belonged to summer. That is no longer the case. The trend has become so widespread that many cafes now see cold drinks outsell hot ones, even during cooler months. Search trends support this shift. People began looking for cold brew beans and concentrates early in 2025 and continued well beyond the summer peak.

By fall, cold coffee was not a seasonal trend. It had become a standard.

#### Wellness and lifestyle trends

People want drinks that align with how they feel and live. Cold brew suits a lighter, health conscious mindset, and nitro adds an experience that feels indulgent without being heavy.

cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.title: Effective Coffee Shop Marketing Strategies
cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.content:
# Effective Coffee Shop Marketing Strategies

March 16, 2025 (Published: July 22, 2024)

Image of a marketing plan

Want to succeed in today’s competitive specialty coffee market? Develop a marketing plan! Effective coffee shop marketing strategies can attract new customers, keep them coming back and foster customer loyalty.

Your potential customers have many choices for their daily latte, café mocha, espresso, or cold brew. Depending on your local market, you may be up against specialty coffee chains, franchises, other independent coffee shops – and even truck stops. Your goal? Creating a loyal customer base that prioritizes visiting your shop. [...] ### Product

Your product offering is the backbone of your business. Ensure you offer high-quality coffee and distinctive options to cater to different tastes and preferences.

 Quality Control: Invest in comprehensive training for yourself and your team, then regularly taste-test your offerings to ensure consistency.

 Menu Diversity: Offer seasonal specials, specialty drinks and alternative milk options to attract a broader audience.

 Food Pairings: Complement your coffee with locally sourced pastries and other food items. When determining your food product mix, consider workflow efficiencies and waste control.

### Price

Pricing can significantly impact your customer’s perception of value.

 Competitive Analysis: Research local competitors to understand pricing trends. [...] Offer new or “limited time only” options to create the perception that there is always something new in your coffee shop. Rolling out weekly specials on the same day each week or a limited-time seasonal favorite encourages a sense of urgency for customers to stop in and try it before it’s gone!
 Use a flat discount amount rather than a percentage of the sale. For any discount, use a tracking method, such as a POS discount code or a QR code.
 Bundle your product with other local products. For example, consider partnering with a local florist on a special holiday to offer pre-order sales on a latte and floral bouquet bundle to be prepared and picked up during certain hours.

cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.title: Seasonal Marketing Strategies for Coffee Shops: Boost your Sales!
cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.content:
2. Host Sip-tacular Events and Workshops

Brewing up a sense of community is just as important as brewing great coffee. Hosting seasonal events like a gingerbread house workshop during the holidays or a cold brew class in the summer can create buzz and draw in crowds. These events not only enhance customer loyalty but also give you the opportunity to showcase your expertise and create memorable experiences for your guests.

Pro Tip: Promote these events through your social media channels and email newsletters to maximize attendance and build anticipation.

3. Sprinkle Seasonal Decor All Around [...] 6. Share the Love on Social Media

In today’s digital age, a strong social media presence is a must. Use your platforms to showcase your seasonal menu items, behind-the-scenes peeks at your decor updates, and photos from your sip-tacular events. Engage your audience with polls about their favorite seasonal drinks, and encourage them to share their own photos and experiences at your shop.

Pro Tip: Create a branded hashtag for your coffee shop, like #BrewWithDelight, to encourage customers to share their experiences online.

7. Celebrate Coffee Holidays and Local Festivities [...] 4. Froth Up Seasonal Promotions and Discounts

Who doesn’t love a good deal? Seasonal promotions like “Buy One, Get One Free” on holiday lattes or discounts on gift cards can give your sales a serious boost. These limited-time offers create excitement and encourage customers to indulge a little more often, whether they’re treating themselves or buying gifts for friends and family.

Pro Tip: Introduce a “seasonal punch card” that rewards customers after they purchase a certain number of seasonal drinks, keeping them coming back for more.

5. Blend with Local Businesses

cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.title: Making coffee popular again – the great marketing campaign
cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.content:
But beyond these strategies, could a marketing campaign to promote coffee as a cornerstone of community and culture be the simple answer, or at least the launchpad?

Pop star Sabrina Carpenter endorsing Dunkin, Hollywood actor Ryan Reynolds endorsing STōK Cold Brew, and social media influencer Emma Chamberlain launching her own coffee brand are all examples of celebrities who have successfully put coffee back on the map – not in in terms of product sales, but culturally with the many. [...] This strategy borrows from the luxury playbook. As with watches or whiskey, perceived rarity and provenance can drive demand even in tight economies. If coffee becomes more of an experience – like wine tasting or chocolate pairing – it may reclaim its centrality by elevating itself beyond the quotidian.

Digital innovation may also play a role. Brands like Cometeer tried betting on frozen specialty coffee pods for home use, while Jot and others are marketing concentrated brews for mixable convenience. The next generation of campaigns may fuse convenience, quality and design – packaging daily coffee as both effortless and elevated. [...] “I believe these messages resonate with many consumers, while lifestyle advertising with celebrities, social situations, and relatable scenarios are also relatable to many consumers.  It is the task of marketing geniuses to know which advertising will resonate with which consumers, and if those consumers are the target market for the coffee brand or coffee product.”

The latest campaigns arise in response to a very real trend: coffee, a mature product, though still registering a healthy current value, is seeing volume growth slow or stall. In high-income countries, saturation, economic uncertainty and shifting generational preferences have led to stagnation for many. Coffee runs the risk of no longer being a cultural default.

cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.title: How to Enhance Your Coffee Sales During the Holidays
cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.content:
This is also a wonderful time of year to make gift-giving easier for your customers by bundling your merchandise into sample kits and coffee gift baskets. Holiday-themed packages that include mugs, gift cards, and products with your business logo on them are a great addition to a customer’s in-store beverage purchase. Furthermore, you can promote a higher volume of coffee sales and increase customer retention by offering BOGOs and other quantity discount promotions.

### Online Shopping & Pick-Up [...] ### Key Takeaways

This holiday season is sure to be different than the last, as more consumers are eager to make up for the lost time with family and friends. Many people are looking forward to enjoying a cup of their favorite beverages, like a pumpkin spice latte, peppermint mocha, or hot chocolate, next to loved ones again. By tapping into flavor trends that give your customers a heart-warming holiday beverage experience, experimenting with seasonal ingredients for limited-time offers, and promoting gift cards, gift baskets, and brand merchandise in person and online, your coffee sales will be set up for success during this holiday season. [...] ### Holiday Beverage Trends

Holiday-themed coffee beverages are one of the most accessible and heart-warming ways for people to get into the spirit. Peppermint and pumpkin spice tend to be the leading holiday flavors, but there are several other trending options this year that your baristas should experiment with like eggnog, gingerbread, marshmallow, chestnut, and peanut butter. Preparing these as both hot and cold beverages with preparation methods varying from lattes to mochas to frappuccinos is a great way to diversify your menu now and in the future.

cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.title: 12 Café Marketing Ideas | Seven Miles Coffee Roasters
cold brew coffee Easter holiday competit.content:
- Find a prize – like a Mother’s Day lunch, or a gift hamper.
- To enter, customers need to tag someone else they want to win the prize (in our example, their mum) and include 3 words that describe why she’s the best.
- Include a closing date and explain how the winner will be announced.
- Share the post on social, and add a sign in store to promote the competition.
Here’s an example of a competition we ran earlier this year
> View this post on Instagram
> [...] One simple way they can do that is through a coffee card.

Depending on the partner, you either can offer them ‘free coffee’ cards to refer their customers to you or sell them the cards at a discounted rate.

In my case, we had several local real estate agents who were happy to spend hundreds of dollars a month to shout their clients a free coffee.

## 12. Social Competition

We all love a competition.

It’s also a great way to get people sharing your business on social. Here’s one example of how you can run a social media competition for your café or coffee shop: [...] Just be aware that Facebook & Instagram have different rules on what you can & can’t do with competitions like this – so be sure to check the details before posting.

## What about café marketing strategy?

Hopefully, some of the ideas on this list have given you some inspiration for your business.

However, it’s worth pointing out that none of these tactics are a substitute for putting together a proper marketing strategy for your business.

An effective strategy starts by first understanding the specific customers you’re targeting, and then aligning what you offer & what you say (your message) with their needs.

# 69% of Consumers Plan to Celebrate Easter, 53% to Buy Candy, Numerator Reports

03.19.24

SEE ALL RELEASES

Gen Z Three Times as Likely to Order Takeout on Easter; Millennials Most Likely to Give Gifts

CHICAGO, March 19, 2023 (GlobeNewswire) – Numerator, a data and tech company serving the market research space, has released new data on Easter 2024 consumer behavior, sourced from verified purchase data and a sentiment survey of over 5,000 consumers on their 2024 holiday intentions. Overall, 69% of consumers plan to celebrate Easter this year, and it is the second-most popular holiday for purchasing candy (after Halloween), with 53% of celebrators intending to buy candy.

Consumer Sentiment Findings Include: [...] + 1 in 5 Easter celebrators will purchase gifts for the holiday. Millennials are more likely to buy gifts than other generations (26% vs. 23% for all consumers).
  + Gen Z is the most likely generation to purchase alcohol. Gen Z is twice as likely to buy alcoholic beverages for the holiday (28% vs. 14% for all consumers). They plan to purchase beer (75%), wine (25%), hard seltzers (25%), champagne or sparkling wine (25%).
  + Grocery stores will see the most Easter shoppers. Consumers plan to spend less than $100 on their Easter celebrations, and they plan to spend their dollars at grocery stores (57%), big box stores like Target or Walmart (53%), and online retailers like Amazon (21%).

coffee consumers Easter holiday audience.title: The Ultimate Coffee Gift Guide For Every Budget [2024 Holiday ...
coffee consumers Easter holiday audience.content:
Written by: Garrett Oden

![The Ultimate Coffee Gift Guide For Every Budget [2024 Holiday Edition]](//www.javapresse.com/cdn/shop/articles/coffee_holiday_gift_guide_javapresse.jpg?v=1570465166&width=780)

Coffee gifts are beloved, relatively safe, and high-value gifts to give your special friends and loved ones. The problem is, everyone has different coffee tastes, habits, and equipment - and that makes buying the best coffee gifts a little stressful.

Let’s make this fun. Forget the cliches. Forget the gift cards.

We created this Ultimate Coffee Gift Guide to help you—no matter your budget—find meaningful ways to demonstrate love and care to the coffee lovers in your life. [...] Most people buy gift coffee bags weeks in advance.

Please, please don’t do this.

Coffee is only fresh for TWO weeks after it’s roasted—and only twenty minutes after it’s ground. If you buy a bag of coffee two weeks before the gifting date, you’ll be giving away coffee that’s already beyond its prime (which means the flavors aren’t nearly as vibrant).

coffee consumers Easter holiday audience.title: Some holiday gifts can backfire – leading to hurt feelings, bad reviews
coffee consumers Easter holiday audience.content:
Shift promotions for self-improvement items like fitness equipment or self-help books from November and December to January, when consumers are focused on their own New Year’s resolutions.
 Rethink pricing incentives during gifting seasons. If retailers discount both self-improvement and leisure products for the holidays, Chapman suggests spotlighting the latter instead.
 Humanize review requests. When companies solicit feedback, adding a personal touch such as a note signed by a real employee (“Thanks for your review! – Alex, Founder”) can reduce consumers’ negativity.
 Offer small incentives for reviews. Even a modest gift card can increase positivity and reduce the likelihood of low ratings.

cold brew coffee Easter best performing.title: 10 Best Performing Ad Hooks: Examples That Actually Work
cold brew coffee Easter best performing.content:
Dynamic typography: Use large, animated text in the center, not just subtitles at the bottom.
 Problem/solution visuals: Show the "Before" state right away. It delivers the message instantly.
 The "secret" angle: Display bold phrases like “The one secret experts won’t tell you” to tempt viewers to turn sound on.

### Format UGC specifically for authenticity on Meta platforms

User-Generated Content works best if it looks native. Polished videos reduce trust. [...] Urgency hooks drive immediate action with commands. Use "Stop scrolling!", "Do this now if you want to [achieve result]", "Drop everything and try this!", or "Follow these 3 steps to [desired goal]."
 Social proof and FOMO hooks establish instant trust. Leverage phrases like "Over 10,000 people are already doing this, why not you?", "You'll regret missing this trend in 2026", "Don't get left behind, this is the future of [industry]", or "Everyone's raving about this product, here's why." [...] The 3-Second Rule: Audiences scroll quickly. Start with a powerful visual or a "pattern interrupt" in the first frame to stop the thumb.
 Curiosity gaps: Use hooks that provoke immediate questions, like “I cannot believe I didn’t discover this earlier…” or “What happens next will shock you…” These increase retention significantly.
 Algorithm advantage: Platforms reward immediate watch time. Fast-paced hooks boost your delivery, lowering CPMs and increasing reach.

### Optimize visual hooks for sound-off viewing using dynamic text overlays

Many viewers watch with the sound off. If your hook depends on audio, you lose them immediately.

cold brew coffee Easter best performing.title: Ad Hooks That Scale: Turning 1 Winning Angle Into 20 Performing ...
cold brew coffee Easter best performing.content:
“Volume is very important here – we pump out dozens of versions a week with different scenes, thumbnails, different voices. Each platform needs its own format anyway, so we tighten or reframe for TikTok versus YouTube versus LinkedIn.” – Paulina Gębczyk, Marketing Lead at CrustLab

#### 2. Incremental variation outperforms net-new concepts

Paid social platforms reward small creative changes (new intros, visuals, or CTAs) more than full script rewrites. Iterating ad hooks enables faster testing cycles, making it easier to identify improvements in thumbstop rate, hold rate, and CTR. [...] #### Hooks deliver that angle in the first seconds

Ad Hooks are repeatable expression patterns – questions, contradictions, POV opens, and visual reveals. hThey translate the angle into an attention-grabbing moment. Because platforms reward strong early engagement, hooks influence distribution and watch-time more than any other part of the ad.

#### Why teams confuse them?

Teams mix hooks and angles when they assume the opening line is the angle. In reality, the angle is the strategic idea; the hook is one of many ways to express it.

When teams separate the two, they gain the ability to produce dozens of high-performing variations without changing the underlying story.

ad hooks variations

## Why Scaling Hooks Matters [...] Billo

## Ways to Multiply a Single Idea

Short‑form platforms reward structural variation. Once a winning angle is identified, you can multiply it across different ad hooks that trigger different curiosity patterns while keeping the core message intact.

Some of the options may be to:

“I’d take the original ad copy and just change the pronouns. For instance, “I struggled with X, and this product helped me achieve Y” becomes “Do you struggle with X? This product can help you achieve Y.” – Janelle Warner, Co-director at Born Social

## Metrics to Track When Testing Hooks

Evaluating ad hooks effectively comes down to watching the earliest signals of attention and intent. These reveal within hours whether a variation has real potential or is already losing momentum.

cold brew coffee Easter best performing.title: How to market canned cold brew coffee to your customers
cold brew coffee Easter best performing.content:
Similarly, ensure tailor your messaging to the channel you’re using. For example, newsletters should have clear CTAs which drive potential customers to the right product, while videos on social media can be more about positioning your brand and creating a clear and cohesive identity.

Canned cold brew coffee does have qualities which are inherently suitable to specific marketing channels, however. For instance, the foamy head and cascade have an “Instagrammable” quality that is extremely popular with younger consumers.

However, Brandon says he has one key tactic that he thinks is an unmissable opportunity: free samples. [...] “We know that these consumers like transparency and the humanisation of their products. So, using a responsibly sourced coffee with a story could absolutely capture the attention of these demographics,” Brandon adds. “Don’t just focus on the taste or quality, but rather the hands behind the product.”

Friends enjoying a nitro-infused canned cold brew coffee

## Plan your overall marketing strategy

Finally, as with any good marketing campaign, make sure to have a plan to reach your target demographics.

“You should meet the consumer where they’re at rather than relying on happenstance,” Brandon says. “Utilise social media, event marketing, and alternative distribution channels – marketing pop-ups on campuses or at concerts and other events can drive sales.” [...] “I believe the mouthfeel contributes to the perception of sweetness and approachability,” Brandon says. “For some people, it’s far easier to drink than a hot coffee or a shot of espresso. It’s an easy delivery of caffeine without some of the bitterness or perceivable acidity that you might get with another brewing method.”

Nitro-infusion also gives the beverage a creamy texture and, when poured, creates a distinctive layered aesthetic.

cold brew coffee Easter best performing.title: Specialty Coffee & Tea Influencer Marketing Campaigns ...
cold brew coffee Easter best performing.content:
Diverse Content for Maximum Reach: Each influencer brought their own unique spin to the content, creating a library of varying perspectives that kept things fresh and engaging. Whether it was a TikTok showcasing a new iced coffee recipe or a cozy Instagram shot of a morning ritual, Chameleon ensured they had a steady stream of content to share across all channels—owned, earned, and paid.

A post shared by Nate “JJ WOLF” Cantalupo | Creative Marketing & Video (@jjwolfcreative)

### Results That Keep On Brewing

### 4. User-generated content (UGC)

Encourage your audience to share their Easter celebrations by asking them to post photos or videos using a branded hashtag. By featuring the best user-generated content on your page, you can create a sense of community and appreciation among your audience.

### 5. Countdown to Easter

Plan a series of Easter-themed images, fun facts, or challenges as a way to count down to the big day. You could also customise a branded game and pair it with a customised digital countdown calendar that users click through to from your posts.

## Customize Easter Messaging

On social media, 52% female and 48% male users engage in distinct yet complementary conversations reflecting common interest in food, creativity, and community during the Easter season. Content by men focuses on main dish preparations like grilled lamb or ham paired with craft beverages, along with organizing creative outdoor egg hunts and hunting for the best seasonal deals. Meanwhile, women emphasize festive dessert baking, DIY crafts, and planning warm, family-centered celebrations. Companies and brands can tailor festive messaging by age and gender to maximize engagement around their offerings on social media platform X.

On average, news articles achieved a significant reach of 4.29M per mention. The social media buzz around indulgence and gifting increased in mid-March due to several factors, including limited-edition launches, the influence of social media influencers, and attractive festive packaging. Easter coffee viral content topics socia.content:
## Why the Coffee Industry is Constantly Changing

Trends come and go. Anyone looking to discover coffee industry trends just needs to check social media. Thanks to platforms like Instagram and TikTok, new coffee trends spread faster than ever before. The growing willingness of coffee fans to experiment has also contributed to coffee being much more than just a morning wake-up drink. Many people are searching for unique taste experiences and enjoy trying new combinations. Moreover, societal developments, such as increasing health consciousness and environmental awareness, influence the choice of coffee beverages. Coffee providers are responding with creative coffee trends that are both flavorful and sustainable.

## Pistachio in Coffee – Instagram & TikTok’s Coffee Trends 2025 [...] TikTok and Instagram are well-established trend drivers. Alongside fun dance videos, new food trends frequently emerge on these platforms. The TikTok trend with “Dubai Chocolate” started in 2024 and continues to fascinate people this year. This also led to the new coffee trend of the year: pistachio in coffee! But how can you prepare this unique coffee idea at home, and can you discover new coffee creations at Coffee-Bike and rent the mobile coffee bar?

### How Pistachio Became the New Coffee Trend [...] ### How Pistachio Became the New Coffee Trend

The TikTok coffee trend, just like the Instagram coffee trend, originated in Dubai. In mid-2024, “Dubai Chocolate” gained popularity through social media, but it was initially unavailable anywhere. Influencers demonstrated how to easily prepare the hyped chocolate at home. This led to numerous new variations, including the new pistachio coffee creation.

### Making Pistachio Coffee at Home – Here’s How

Want to try this trending coffee at home? Here’s what you need:

 1 cup of milk or a plant-based alternative
 1 tbsp pistachio paste
 Certified organic espresso or coffee beans for 1 espresso or a strong cup of coffee
 A little honey or vanilla syrup to taste
